title: The negativity core of a 1+1D massless scalar quantum field
authors: Jason Pye, Atharva Hingane, Robert H. Jonsson
abstract: Vacuum entanglement is a fundamental feature of quantum field theory exhibiting rich structure that is not completely understood. Here, we provide a complete characterization of the entanglement between two bounded spacelike-separated regions in a (1+1)-dimensional free massless real scalar field. Employing Gaussian state methods, we analytically compute the logarithmic negativity and construct closed-form solutions for the localized modes carrying it, called negativity cores. These results deepen our understanding of quantum fields and suggest extensions to higher dimensions and fermionic fields.
summary: This paper provides an analytical solution for the entanglement between two disjoint intervals in a 1+1D massless scalar field. It identifies 'negativity cores,' which are the specific localized modes that constitute the entanglement. The results bridge the gap between local field theory and the geometric structure of quantum correlations.
main_problem: The paper aims to provide a complete characterization of the entanglement structure between two bounded, spacelike-separated regions in a (1+1)-dimensional free massless real scalar field, specifically identifying the localized modes that carry the entanglement.
main_result: The authors derive closed-form analytical solutions for the logarithmic negativity and identify the 'negativity cores,' which are the specific localized field modes responsible for the entanglement.
method: The study employs Gaussian state methods and a framework based on the Kähler structure of Gaussian states, reducing the problem to a boundary value problem in the complex plane involving the diagonalization of an integral operator.
model_or_system: A (1+1)-dimensional free massless real scalar quantum field in its vacuum state, where the system consists of two disjoint, bounded intervals on the real axis.
key_observables: Logarithmic negativity (En) and negativity cores (localized smearing functions/modes).
important_parameters: The cross ratio (eta) characterizing the geometry, the ratio of separation to interval length (d/l), and the large separation (eta -> 0) and small separation (eta -> 1) regimes.
main_assumptions: Observers are assumed to couple only to the derivatives of the field rather than the field amplitude, and the field is assumed to be in its vacuum state.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the spatial localization of the negativity cores by displaying the real part of the smearing functions for the first three modes.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of vacuum entanglement, defines the (1+1)D scalar field model, presents the analytical derivation of the negativity and its mode decomposition using Gaussian state methods, validates the results with a lattice-based numerical model, and discusses asymptotic scaling regimes.
why_interesting: This work provides fundamental insights into the spatial structure of vacuum entanglement and the identification of specific field modes that carry correlations, which is highly relevant for understanding entanglement in continuous variable systems and quantum field theory.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
